hooks.command: file_path=$(echo "$MCP_TOOL_INPUT" | grep -o '"file_path":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4); case "$file_path" in *.ts|*.tsx|*.js|*.jsx) npm run lint 2>&1 | tail -20 ;; esac
file_path=$(echo "$MCP_TOOL_INPUT" | grep -o '"file_path":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4); case "$file_path" in */lib/*.ts|*/app/page.tsx) npm run test 2>&1 | tail -15 ;; esac

file_path=$(echo "$MCP_TOOL_INPUT" | grep -o '"file_path":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4); case "$file_path" in */src/data/*) echo 'BLOCK: Data files are generated. Use npm run import or the scrape script instead.' && exit 1 ;; esac

hooks.prompt:
You are an architecture drift detector. Check if this code change violates any existing Architecture Decision Records.

The tool input is: $ARGUMENTS

Read ALL files in docs/decisions/ (skip template.md). For each ADR, understand the decision that was made, then check if the proposed code change contradicts it. Think about what kind of code changes would violate each decision.

If no violation is detected, respond with exactly: PASS
If a violation is detected, respond with: WARN: [which ADR] - [what the violation is]. This change may be intentional — consider updating the ADR if the decision has changed.
